meaning: adj.选任的,有选举权的,随意选择的n.选修课程
examples: Buchanan has never held elective office.
Most of the students of our class take Japanese as an elective course.
To learn more about Chinese culture, Jack has decided to take Chinese folk music as an elective course.
She was the only girl to pick caving as a sports elective in grade school.
This epidemic of uncertain care is an unintended outgrowth of a decades-long boom in elective surgery.
